The theory explains why scaling alone cannot eliminate persistent human-aligned errors and characterizes conditions under which auxiliary non-human signals (e.g., retrieval, program execution, tools) increase effective supervision capacity and collapse the floor by restoring information about the latent target. Experiments on real preference data, synthetic known-target tasks, and externally verifiable benchmarks confirm the predicted structural signatures: human-only supervision exhibits a persistent floor, while sufficiently informative auxiliary channels strictly reduce or eliminate excess error.

zh.title: 人类监督作为信息瓶颈：人类引导学习中错误下限的统一理论
zh.summary: 本论文提出了一个统一的理论，表明人类监督渠道施加的结构性限制在人工智能系统中造成持久的错误下限，与模型规模无关。它证明了检索和程序执行等辅助非人类信号可以增加监督容量并消除这些错误上限。

fr.title: La supervision humaine comme goulot d'étranglement informatif : une théorie unifiée des planchers d'erreur dans l'apprentissage guidé par l'homme
fr.summary: Cet article présente une théorie unifiée montrant que les canaux de supervision humaine imposent des limitations structurelles créant des planchers d'erreur persistants dans les systèmes d'IA, indépendamment de la mise à l'échelle du modèle. Il démontre que les signaux auxiliaires non-humains tels que la récupération et l'exécution de programme peuvent augmenter la capacité de supervision et éliminer ces plafonds d'erreur.

de.title: Menschliche Überwachung als Informationsengpass: Eine einheitliche Theorie von Fehleruntergrenzen beim menschengeführten Lernen
de.summary: Dieses Papier präsentiert eine einheitliche Theorie, die zeigt, dass menschliche Aufsichtskanäle strukturelle Einschränkungen aufzwingen, die in KI-Systemen zu anhaltenden Fehleruntergrenzen führen, unabhängig von der Modellskalierung. Es demonstriert, dass zusätzliche nicht-menschliche Signale wie Abruf und Programmausführung die Aufsichtskapazität erhöhen und diese Fehlerobergrenzen eliminieren können.

es.title: La supervisión humana como cuello de botella informativo: Una teoría unificada de los pisos de error en el aprendizaje guiado por humanos
es.summary: Este artículo presenta una teoría unificada que muestra que los canales de supervisión humana imponen limitaciones estructurales que crean pisos de error persistentes en sistemas de IA, independientemente del escalado del modelo. Demuestra que las señales auxiliares no humanas como la recuperación y la ejecución de programas pueden aumentar la capacidad de supervisión y eliminar estos techos de error.

title: SALIENT: Frequency-Aware Paired Diffusion for Controllable Long-Tail CT Detection
summary: SALIENT uses wavelet-domain diffusion to synthesize paired lesion-masking volumes for augmenting rare lesion detection in medical CT imaging. The frequency-aware approach provides interpretable attribute control and demonstrates substantial improvements in detection performance across low-prevalence, low-volume scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2602.23447v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Detection of rare lesions in whole-body CT is fundamentally limited by extreme class imbalance and low target-to-volume ratios, producing precision collapse despite high AUROC. Synthetic augmentation with diffusion models offers promise, yet pixel-space diffusion is computationally expensive, and existing mask-conditioned approaches lack controllable attribute-level regulation and paired supervision for accountable training. We introduce SALIENT, a mask-conditioned wavelet-domain diffusion framework that synthesizes paired lesion-masking volumes for controllable CT augmentation under long-tail regimes. Instead of denoising in pixel space, SALIENT performs structured diffusion over discrete wavelet coefficients, explicitly separating low-frequency brightness from high-frequency structural detail. Learnable frequency-aware objectives disentangle target and background attributes (structure, contrast, edge fidelity), enabling interpretable and stable optimization. A 3D VAE generates diverse volumetric lesion masks, and a semi-supervised teacher produces paired slice-level pseudo-labels for downstream mask-guided detection. SALIENT improves generative realism, as reflected by higher MS-SSIM (0.63 to 0.83) and lower FID (118.4 to 46.5). In a separate downstream evaluation, SALIENT-augmented training improves long-tail detection performance, yielding disproportionate AUPRC gains across low prevalences and target-to-volume ratios. Optimal synthetic ratios shift from 2x to 4x as labeled seed size decreases, indicating a seed-dependent augmentation regime under low-label conditions. SALIENT demonstrates that frequency-aware diffusion enables controllable, computationally efficient precision rescue in long-tail CT detection.

zh.title: SALIENT：频率感知配对扩散用于可控长尾CT检测
zh.summary: SALIENT使用小波域扩散来合成配对的病灶屏蔽体积，以增强医学CT成像中的罕见病灶检测。频率感知方法提供了可解释的属性控制，并在低患病率、低体积情景中的检测性能方面展示了实质性的改进。

fr.title: SALIENT : Diffusion appariée sensible à la fréquence pour la détection CT à longue traîne contrôlable
fr.summary: SALIENT utilise la diffusion dans le domaine des ondelettes pour synthétiser des volumes de masquage de lésions appariés afin d'augmenter la détection des lésions rares en imagerie CT médicale. L'approche sensible à la fréquence fournit un contrôle d'attributs interprétable et démontre des améliorations substantielles des performances de détection dans les scénarios de faible prévalence et de faible volume.

de.title: SALIENT: Frequenzabhängige gepaarte Diffusion für kontrollierbare Long-Tail-CT-Detektion
de.summary: SALIENT nutzt Wavelet-Domänen-Diffusion zur Synthese gepaarter Läsions-Maskierungsvolumina zur Verbesserung der Erkennung seltener Läsionen in der medizinischen CT-Bildgebung. Der frequenzabhängige Ansatz bietet interpretierbare Attributkontrolle und zeigt erhebliche Verbesserungen der Erkennungsleistung in Szenarien mit niedriger Prävalenz und niedrigem Volumen.

es.title: SALIENT: Difusión por pares consciente de frecuencia para la detección de CT de cola larga controlable
es.summary: SALIENT utiliza difusión en el dominio de wavelets para sintetizar volúmenes de enmascaramiento de lesiones emparejados para aumentar la detección de lesiones raras en imágenes CT médicas. El enfoque consciente de frecuencia proporciona control de atributos interpretable y demuestra mejoras sustanciales en el desempeño de detección en escenarios de baja prevalencia y bajo volumen.

title: BiKA: Kolmogorov-Arnold-Network-inspired Ultra Lightweight Neural Network Hardware Accelerator
summary: BiKA is a multiply-free neural network accelerator inspired by Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks, requiring only comparators and accumulators for computation. FPGA prototyping demonstrates 27-51% reduction in hardware resources compared to existing quantized and binarized accelerators while maintaining competitive accuracy.
content:
arXiv:2602.23455v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Lightweight neural network accelerators are essential for edge devices with limited resources and power constraints. While quantization and binarization can efficiently reduce hardware cost, they still rely on the conventional Artificial Neural Network (ANN) computation pattern. The recently proposed Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) presents a novel network paradigm built on learnable nonlinear functions. However, it is computationally expensive for hardware deployment. Inspired by KAN, we propose BiKA, a multiply-free architecture that replaces nonlinear functions with binary, learnable thresholds, introducing an extremely lightweight computational pattern that requires only comparators and accumulators. Our FPGA prototype on Ultra96-V2 shows that BiKA reduces hardware resource usage by 27.73% and 51.54% compared with binarized and quantized neural network systolic array accelerators, while maintaining competitive accuracy. BiKA provides a promising direction for hardware-friendly neural network design on edge devices.

zh.title: BiKA：受Kolmogorov-Arnold网络启发的超轻量级神经网络硬件加速器
zh.summary: BiKA是一个受Kolmogorov-Arnold网络启发的无乘法神经网络加速器，计算仅需要比较器和累加器。FPGA原型设计证明了与现有量化和二值化加速器相比，硬件资源减少了27-51%，同时保持了竞争性的准确性。

fr.title: BiKA : Accélérateur matériel de réseau de neurones ultra-léger inspiré par les réseaux de Kolmogorov-Arnold
fr.summary: BiKA est un accélérateur de réseau de neurones sans multiplication inspiré par les réseaux de Kolmogorov-Arnold, nécessitant uniquement des comparateurs et des accumulateurs pour le calcul. Le prototypage FPGA démontre une réduction de 27-51% des ressources matérielles par rapport aux accélérateurs quantifiés et binarisés existants tout en maintenant une précision compétitive.

de.title: BiKA: Von Kolmogorov-Arnold-Netzwerken inspirierter Ultra-leichter Neural-Network-Hardware-Beschleuniger
de.summary: BiKA ist ein multiplikationsfreier Neural-Network-Beschleuniger, inspiriert von Kolmogorov-Arnold-Netzwerken, der nur Vergleicher und Akkumulatoren für die Berechnung benötigt. FPGA-Prototyping zeigt eine Reduktion der Hardware-Ressourcen um 27-51% im Vergleich zu bestehenden quantisierten und binarisierten Beschleunigern bei gleichzeitig wettbewerbsfähiger Genauigkeit.

es.title: BiKA: Acelerador de hardware de red neuronal ultraligero inspirado en redes de Kolmogorov-Arnold
es.summary: BiKA es un acelerador de red neuronal libre de multiplicación inspirado en redes de Kolmogorov-Arnold, que requiere solo comparadores y acumuladores para el cálculo. El prototipado FPGA demuestra una reducción del 27-51% en los recursos de hardware en comparación con los aceleradores cuantificados y binarizados existentes mientras se mantiene una precisión competitiva.

title: Optimization of Edge Directions and Weights for Mixed Guidance Graphs in Lifelong Multi-Agent Path Finding
summary: This paper extends Guidance Graph Optimization by incorporating edge direction optimization alongside weight optimization in Mixed Guidance Graph Optimization (MGGO). Two methods are presented—phase-based and Quality Diversity algorithms—improving guidance in lifelong multi-agent path finding scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2602.23468v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Multi-Agent Path Finding (MAPF) aims to move agents from their start to goal vertices on a graph. Lifelong MAPF (LMAPF) continuously assigns new goals to agents as they complete current ones. To guide agents' movement in LMAPF, prior works have proposed Guidance Graph Optimization (GGO) methods to optimize a guidance graph, which is a bidirected weighted graph whose directed edges represent moving and waiting actions with edge weights being action costs. Higher edge weights represent higher action costs. However, edge weights only provide soft guidance. An edge with a high weight only discourages agents from using it, instead of prohibiting agents from traversing it. In this paper, we explore the need to incorporate edge directions optimization into GGO, providing strict guidance. We generalize GGO to Mixed Guidance Graph Optimization (MGGO), presenting two MGGO methods capable of optimizing both edge weights and directions. The first optimizes edge directions and edge weights in two phases separately. The second applies Quality Diversity algorithms to optimize a neural network capable of generating edge directions and weights. We also incorporate traffic patterns relevant to edge directions into a GGO method, making it capable of generating edge-direction-aware guidance graphs.

zh.title: 混合引导图中边方向和权重的优化在终身多智能体路径规划中的应用
zh.summary: 本文通过在混合引导图优化（MGGO）中并入边方向优化来扩展引导图优化。本文提出了两种方法——基于阶段的算法和质量多样性算法——以改进终身多智能体路径规划场景中的引导。

fr.title: Optimisation des directions et poids des arêtes pour les graphes de guidage mixtes dans la recherche de chemin multi-agents permanente
fr.summary: Cet article étend l'optimisation du graphe de guidage en incorporant l'optimisation de la direction des arêtes aux côtés de l'optimisation des poids dans l'optimisation du graphe de guidage mixte (MGGO). Deux méthodes sont présentées—des algorithmes basés sur les phases et de diversité qualitative—améliorant le guidage dans les scénarios de recherche de chemin multi-agents permanente.

de.title: Optimierung von Kantenrichtungen und Gewichten für gemischte Anleitungsgraphen in dauerhafter Multi-Agent-Pfadfindung
de.summary: Dieses Papier erweitert die Guidance Graph Optimization durch die Integration der Kantenrichtungsoptimierung neben der Gewichtsoptimierung in der Mixed Guidance Graph Optimization (MGGO). Zwei Methoden werden präsentiert—phasenbasierte und Quality-Diversity-Algorithmen—die die Anleitung in Szenarios der lebenslangen Multi-Agent-Pfadfindung verbessern.

es.title: Optimización de direcciones y pesos de aristas para gráficos de orientación mixta en búsqueda de caminos multiagente permanente
es.summary: Este artículo extiende la Optimización del Gráfico de Orientación mediante la incorporación de la optimización de dirección de aristas junto con la optimización de pesos en la Optimización del Gráfico de Orientación Mixta (MGGO). Se presentan dos métodos—algoritmos basados en fases y algoritmos de Diversidad de Calidad—mejorando la orientación en escenarios de búsqueda de caminos multiagente permanente.

title: TaCarla: A comprehensive benchmarking dataset for end-to-end autonomous driving
summary: TaCarla provides a comprehensive autonomous driving benchmark of 2.85 million frames from CARLA Leaderboard 2.0 scenarios supporting perception, planning, and prediction tasks. It addresses dataset limitations through diverse behavioral coverage and proper closed-loop evaluation infrastructure for end-to-end autonomous driving research.
content:
arXiv:2602.23499v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Collecting a high-quality dataset is a critical task that demands meticulous attention to detail, as overlooking certain aspects can render the entire dataset unusable. Autonomous driving challenges remain a prominent area of research, requiring further exploration to enhance the perception and planning performance of vehicles. However, existing datasets are often incomplete. For instance, datasets that include perception information generally lack planning data, while planning datasets typically consist of extensive driving sequences where the ego vehicle predominantly drives forward, offering limited behavioral diversity. In addition, many real datasets struggle to evaluate their models, especially for planning tasks, since they lack a proper closed-loop evaluation setup. The CARLA Leaderboard 2.0 challenge, which provides a diverse set of scenarios to address the long-tail problem in autonomous driving, has emerged as a valuable alternative platform for developing perception and planning models in both open-loop and closed-loop evaluation setups. Nevertheless, existing datasets collected on this platform present certain limitations. Some datasets appear to be tailored primarily for limited sensor configuration, with particular sensor configurations. To support end-to-end autonomous driving research, we have collected a new dataset comprising over 2.85 million frames using the CARLA simulation environment for the diverse Leaderboard 2.0 challenge scenarios. Our dataset is designed not only for planning tasks but also supports dynamic object detection, lane divider detection, centerline detection, traffic light recognition, prediction tasks and visual language action models . Furthermore, we demonstrate its versatility by training various models using our dataset. Moreover, we also provide numerical rarity scores to understand how rarely the current state occurs in the dataset.

zh.title: TaCarla：端到端自动驾驶的综合基准测试数据集
zh.summary: TaCarla提供了来自CARLA排行榜2.0场景的综合自动驾驶基准测试，包含285万帧，支持感知、规划和预测任务。它通过多样化的行为覆盖和适当的闭环评估基础设施解决了数据集局限性，用于端到端自动驾驶研究。

fr.title: TaCarla : Un ensemble de données d'évaluation complet pour la conduite autonome de bout en bout
fr.summary: TaCarla fournit un benchmark complet de conduite autonome de 2,85 millions de cadres provenant des scénarios du classement CARLA 2.0 soutenant les tâches de perception, de planification et de prédiction. Il résout les limitations des ensembles de données grâce à une couverture comportementale diversifiée et une infrastructure d'évaluation en boucle fermée appropriée pour la recherche en conduite autonome de bout en bout.

de.title: TaCarla: Ein umfassendes Benchmarking-Dataset für autonomes Fahren von Ende zu Ende
de.summary: TaCarla bietet einen umfassenden Benchmark für autonomes Fahren mit 2,85 Millionen Bildern aus CARLA Leaderboard 2.0-Szenarien, die Wahrnehmungs-, Planungs- und Vorhersageaufgaben unterstützen. Es behebt Datensatzbeschränkungen durch vielfältige Verhaltensabdeckung und angemessene Closed-Loop-Evaluierungsinfrastruktur für die Forschung im autonomen Fahren von Ende zu Ende.

es.title: TaCarla: Un conjunto de datos de referencia integral para conducción autónoma de extremo a extremo
es.summary: TaCarla proporciona un benchmark integral de conducción autónoma de 2,85 millones de fotogramas de escenarios del Leaderboard CARLA 2.0 que admiten tareas de percepción, planificación y predicción. Aborda las limitaciones del conjunto de datos mediante una cobertura de comportamiento diversa e infraestructura de evaluación de bucle cerrado adecuada para la investigación de conducción autónoma de extremo a extremo.

title: FedDAG: Clustered Federated Learning via Global Data and Gradient Integration for Heterogeneous Environments
summary: FedDAG introduces a clustered federated learning framework combining data and gradient similarity for holistic client clustering in heterogeneous environments. Its dual-encoder architecture enables cross-cluster knowledge transfer while preserving cluster specialization, consistently outperforming existing clustered federated learning approaches.
content:
arXiv:2602.23504v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables a group of clients to collaboratively train a model without sharing individual data, but its performance drops when client data are heterogeneous. Clustered FL tackles this by grouping similar clients. However, existing clustered FL approaches rely solely on either data similarity or gradient similarity; however, this results in an incomplete assessment of client similarities. Prior clustered FL approaches also restrict knowledge and representation sharing to clients within the same cluster. This prevents cluster models from benefiting from the diverse client population across clusters. To address these limitations, FedDAG introduces a clustered FL framework, FedDAG, that employs a weighted, class-wise similarity metric that integrates both data and gradient information, providing a more holistic measure of similarity during clustering. In addition, FedDAG adopts a dual-encoder architecture for cluster models, comprising a primary encoder trained on its own clients' data and a secondary encoder refined using gradients from complementary clusters. This enables cross-cluster feature transfer while preserving cluster-specific specialization. Experiments on diverse benchmarks and data heterogeneity settings show that FedDAG consistently outperforms state-of-the-art clustered FL baselines in accuracy.

zh.title: FedDAG：通过全局数据和梯度整合的聚集联邦学习在异构环境中的应用
zh.summary: FedDAG引入了一个聚集联邦学习框架，结合数据和梯度相似度以实现异构环境中的整体客户端聚类。其双编码器架构使跨集群知识转移成为可能，同时保持集群专业化，在现有聚集联邦学习方法中保持一致的优越性能。

fr.title: FedDAG : Apprentissage fédéré regroupé via l'intégration globale de données et de gradients pour les environnements hétérogènes
fr.summary: FedDAG introduit un cadre d'apprentissage fédéré regroupé combinant la similitude des données et des gradients pour un regroupement holistique des clients dans les environnements hétérogènes. Son architecture à double encodeur permet le transfert de connaissances entre clusters tout en préservant la spécialisation des clusters, surpassant régulièrement les approches d'apprentissage fédéré regroupé existantes.

de.title: FedDAG: Geclustertes föderales Lernen durch globale Daten- und Gradientenintegration in heterogenen Umgebungen
de.summary: FedDAG führt ein geclustertes föderales Lernframework ein, das Daten- und Gradientenähnlichkeit für ganzheitliches Client-Clustering in heterogenen Umgebungen kombiniert. Seine Dual-Encoder-Architektur ermöglicht die Übertragung von Wissen zwischen Clustern, während die Cluster-Spezialisierung erhalten bleibt, und übertrifft konsequent bestehende geclusterte föderale Lernansätze.

es.title: FedDAG: Aprendizaje federado agrupado mediante integración global de datos y gradientes para entornos heterogéneos
es.summary: FedDAG introduce un marco de aprendizaje federado agrupado que combina similitud de datos y gradientes para agrupamiento holístico de clientes en entornos heterogéneos. Su arquitectura de doble codificador permite la transferencia de conocimiento entre clústeres mientras preserva la especialización de clústeres, superando consistentemente los enfoques de aprendizaje federado agrupado existentes.

title: SegReg: Latent Space Regularization for Improved Medical Image Segmentation
summary: SegReg applies latent-space regularization to U-Net models for medical image segmentation, encouraging structured embeddings beyond standard output-space losses. It demonstrates consistent improvements in domain generalization and continual learning without additional memory or parameters.
content:
arXiv:2602.23509v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Medical image segmentation models are typically optimised with voxel-wise losses that constrain predictions only in the output space. This leaves latent feature representations largely unconstrained, potentially limiting generalisation. We propose {SegReg}, a latent-space regularisation framework that operates on feature maps of U-Net models to encourage structured embeddings while remaining fully compatible with standard segmentation losses. Integrated with the nnU-Net framework, we evaluate SegReg on prostate, cardiac, and hippocampus segmentation and demonstrate consistent improvements in domain generalisation. Furthermore, we show that explicit latent regularisation improves continual learning by reducing task drift and enhancing forward transfer across sequential tasks without adding memory or any extra parameters. These results highlight latent-space regularisation as a practical approach for building more generalisable and continual-learning-ready models.

zh.title: SegReg：用于改进医学图像分割的隐空间正则化
zh.summary: SegReg对U-Net模型应用隐空间正则化用于医学图像分割，鼓励结构化嵌入超越标准输出空间损失。它在域泛化和持续学习中展示了一致的改进，无需额外的内存或参数。

fr.title: SegReg : Régularisation de l'espace latent pour l'amélioration de la segmentation d'images médicales
fr.summary: SegReg applique la régularisation de l'espace latent aux modèles U-Net pour la segmentation d'images médicales, encourageant les plongements structurés au-delà des pertes d'espace de sortie standard. Il démontre des améliorations constantes dans la généralisation de domaine et l'apprentissage continu sans mémoire ni paramètres supplémentaires.

de.title: SegReg: Latente Raumregularisierung zur verbesserten medizinischen Bildsegmentierung
de.summary: SegReg wendet Latent-Space-Regularisierung auf U-Net-Modelle zur medizinischen Bildsegmentierung an und fördert strukturierte Einbettungen über standardmäßige Ausgaberaumverluste hinaus. Es zeigt konsistente Verbesserungen in der Domänenverallgemeinerung und kontinuierlichem Lernen ohne zusätzlichen Speicher oder Parameter.

es.title: SegReg: Regularización del espacio latente para la mejora de la segmentación de imágenes médicas
es.summary: SegReg aplica regularización del espacio latente a modelos U-Net para la segmentación de imágenes médicas, fomentando incrustaciones estructuradas más allá de pérdidas de espacio de salida estándar. Demuestra mejoras consistentes en generalización de dominio y aprendizaje continuo sin memoria ni parámetros adicionales.

title: Modelling and Simulation of Neuromorphic Datasets for Anomaly Detection in Computer Vision
summary: ANTShapes is a Unity-based simulator for generating customizable neuromorphic vision datasets with configurable 3D scenes and randomly-generated object behaviors. It addresses the scarcity of Dynamic Vision Sensor datasets, enabling researchers to create bespoke datasets for event-based computer vision and anomaly detection.
content:
arXiv:2602.23514v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Limitations on the availability of Dynamic Vision Sensors (DVS) present a fundamental challenge to researchers of neuromorphic computer vision applications. In response, datasets have been created by the research community, but often contain a limited number of samples or scenarios. To address the lack of a comprehensive simulator of neuromorphic vision datasets, we introduce the Anomalous Neuromorphic Tool for Shapes (ANTShapes), a novel dataset simulation framework. Built in the Unity engine, ANTShapes simulates abstract, configurable 3D scenes populated by objects displaying randomly-generated behaviours describing attributes such as motion and rotation. The sampling of object behaviours, and the labelling of anomalously-acting objects, is a statistical process following central limit theorem principles. Datasets containing an arbitrary number of samples can be created and exported from ANTShapes, along with accompanying label and frame data, through the adjustment of a limited number of parameters within the software. ANTShapes addresses the limitations of data availability to researchers of event-based computer vision by allowing for the simulation of bespoke datasets to suit purposes including object recognition and localisation alongside anomaly detection.

zh.title: 用于计算机视觉异常检测的神经形态数据集的建模和仿真
zh.summary: ANTShapes是一个基于Unity的模拟器，用于生成可自定义的神经形态视觉数据集，具有可配置的3D场景和随机生成的对象行为。它解决了动态视觉传感器数据集的稀缺性，使研究人员能够为基于事件的计算机视觉和异常检测创建定制数据集。

fr.title: Modélisation et simulation de jeux de données neuromorphes pour la détection d'anomalies en vision par ordinateur
fr.summary: ANTShapes est un simulateur basé sur Unity pour générer des ensembles de données de vision neuromorphe personnalisables avec des scènes 3D configurables et des comportements d'objets générés aléatoirement. Il résout la pénurie d'ensembles de données de capteurs de vision dynamique, permettant aux chercheurs de créer des ensembles de données sur mesure pour la vision par ordinateur et la détection d'anomalies basées sur les événements.

de.title: Modellierung und Simulation neuromorpher Datensätze zur Anomalieerkennung in der Computervision
de.summary: ANTShapes ist ein Unity-basierter Simulator zur Generierung anpassbarer neuromorpher Vision-Datensätze mit konfigurierbaren 3D-Szenen und zufällig generierten Objektverhalten. Es behebt die Knappheit von Dynamic Vision Sensor-Datensätzen und ermöglicht Forschern, maßgeschneiderte Datensätze für ereignisbasierte Computervision und Anomalieerkennung zu erstellen.

es.title: Modelado y simulación de conjuntos de datos neuromorfo para la detección de anomalías en visión por computadora
es.summary: ANTShapes es un simulador basado en Unity para generar conjuntos de datos de visión neuromorfa personalizables con escenas 3D configurables y comportamientos de objetos generados aleatoriamente. Aborda la escasez de conjuntos de datos de sensores de visión dinámica, permitiendo a los investigadores crear conjuntos de datos personalizados para visión por computadora basada en eventos y detección de anomalías.

title: Humans and LLMs Diverge on Probabilistic Inferences
summary: ProbCOPA, a dataset of 210 handcrafted probabilistic inferences annotated by humans, reveals that state-of-the-art reasoning LLMs fail to reproduce human-like probabilistic judgment distributions. The findings highlight persistent differences between human and LLM reasoning in non-deterministic inference tasks.
content:
arXiv:2602.23546v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Human reasoning often involves working over limited information to arrive at probabilistic conclusions. In its simplest form, this involves making an inference that is not strictly entailed by a premise, but rather only likely given the premise. While reasoning LLMs have demonstrated strong performance on logical and mathematical tasks, their behavior on such open-ended, non-deterministic inferences remains largely unexplored. We introduce ProbCOPA, a dataset of 210 handcrafted probabilistic inferences in English, each annotated for inference likelihood by 25--30 human participants. We find that human responses are graded and varied, revealing probabilistic judgments of the inferences in our dataset. Comparing these judgments with responses from eight state-of-the-art reasoning LLMs, we show that models consistently fail to produce human-like distributions. Finally, analyzing LLM reasoning chains, we find evidence of a common reasoning pattern used to evaluate such inferences. Our findings reveal persistent differences between humans and LLMs, and underscore the need to evaluate reasoning beyond deterministic settings.

zh.title: 人类和大型语言模型在概率推理上的分歧
zh.summary: ProbCOPA是一个包含210个由人类注释的手工制作概率推理的数据集，揭示了最先进的推理大型语言模型未能再现类似人类的概率判断分布。这些发现强调了人类和大型语言模型在非确定性推理任务中推理的持久差异。

fr.title: Les humains et les LLM divergent sur les inférences probabilistes
fr.summary: ProbCOPA est un ensemble de données de 210 inférences probabilistes élaborées à la main annotées par des humains, révélant que les LLM de raisonnement de pointe ne parviennent pas à reproduire les distributions de jugement probabiliste similaires à celles des humains. Les résultats mettent en évidence les différences persistantes entre le raisonnement humain et celui du LLM dans les tâches d'inférence non déterministes.

de.title: Menschen und LLMs unterscheiden sich bei probabilistischen Schlussfolgerungen
de.summary: ProbCOPA ist ein Datensatz mit 210 handgefertigten probabilistischen Schlussfolgerungen, die von Menschen annotiert sind und zeigt, dass hochmoderne Reasoning-LLMs nicht in der Lage sind, menschenähnliche probabilistische Urteilsverteilungen zu reproduzieren. Die Ergebnisse zeigen anhaltende Unterschiede zwischen menschlichem und LLM-Reasoning bei nicht-deterministischen Inferenzaufgaben.

es.title: Humanos y LLMs divergen en inferencias probabilísticas
es.summary: ProbCOPA es un conjunto de datos de 210 inferencias probabilísticas elaboradas a mano anotadas por humanos, que revela que los LLM de razonamiento de última generación no logran reproducir distribuciones de juicio probabilístico similares a las humanas. Los hallazgos destacan diferencias persistentes entre el razonamiento humano y el del LLM en tareas de inferencia no determinista.

title: Rudder: Steering Prefetching in Distributed GNN Training using LLM Agents
summary: Rudder embeds LLM agents into AWS DistDGL to adaptively prefetch remote nodes during large-scale GNN training without static prefetching overhead. The approach leverages emergent in-context learning properties to achieve up to 91% improvement over baseline and 82% over static prefetching methods.
content:
arXiv:2602.23556v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large-scale Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are typically trained by sampling a vertex's neighbors to a fixed distance. Because large input graphs are distributed, training requires frequent irregular communication that stalls forward progress. Moreover, fetched data changes with graph, graph distribution, sample and batch parameters, and caching polices. Consequently, any static prefetching method will miss crucial opportunities to adapt to different dynamic conditions. In this paper, we introduce Rudder, a software module embedded in the state-of-the-art AWS DistDGL framework, to autonomously prefetch remote nodes and minimize communication. Rudder's adaptation contrasts with both standard heuristics and traditional ML classifiers. We observe that the generative AI found in contemporary Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibits emergent properties like In-Context Learning (ICL) for zero-shot tasks, with logical multi-step reasoning. We find this behavior well-suited for adaptive control even with substantial undertraining. Evaluations using standard datasets and unseen configurations on the NERSC Perlmutter supercomputer show up to 91% improvement in end-to-end training performance over baseline DistDGL (no prefetching), and an 82% improvement over static prefetching, reducing communication by over 50%. Our code is available at https://github.com/aishwaryyasarkar/rudder-llm-agent.

zh.title: Rudder: 使用LLM代理在分布式GNN训练中引导预取
zh.summary: Rudder将LLM代理嵌入到AWS DistDGL中，在大规模GNN训练过程中自适应地预取远程节点，避免了静态预取的开销。该方法利用新兴的上下文学习特性，相比基准方法实现了91%的改进，相比静态预取方法实现了82%的改进。

fr.title: Rudder : Guider la préfetching dans l'entraînement GNN distribué en utilisant les agents LLM
fr.summary: Rudder intègre des agents LLM dans AWS DistDGL pour préfetcher de manière adaptative les nœuds distants lors de l'entraînement GNN à grande échelle sans surcharge de préfetching statique. L'approche exploite les propriétés émergentes d'apprentissage en contexte pour atteindre une amélioration de 91% par rapport à la base et 82% par rapport aux méthodes de préfetching statique.

de.title: Rudder: Steuerung des Prefetching beim verteilten GNN-Training mit LLM-Agenten
de.summary: Rudder bettet LLM-Agenten in AWS DistDGL ein, um Remote-Knoten während des großflächigen GNN-Trainings adaptiv vorauszuladen, ohne Overhead durch statisches Prefetching. Der Ansatz nutzt emergente In-Context-Learning-Eigenschaften, um eine Verbesserung von bis zu 91% gegenüber der Basislinie und 82% gegenüber statischen Prefetching-Methoden zu erreichen.

es.title: Rudder: Dirección del prefetching en el entrenamiento GNN distribuido usando agentes LLM
es.summary: Rudder integra agentes LLM en AWS DistDGL para obtener previamente de forma adaptativa nodos remotos durante el entrenamiento GNN a gran escala sin el costo de prefetching estático. El enfoque aprovecha las propiedades emergentes del aprendizaje en contexto para lograr hasta un 91% de mejora sobre la línea base y un 82% sobre los métodos de prefetching estático.

title: Hierarchical Multi-Scale Graph Learning with Knowledge-Guided Attention for Whole-Slide Image Survival Analysis
summary: HMKGN proposes a hierarchical multi-scale graph network for cancer prognostication from whole-slide images, modeling both local cellular interactions and global slide-level features. It consistently outperforms existing multiple-instance learning methods with 10.85% improvement in concordance indices for survival prediction.
content:
arXiv:2602.23557v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We propose a Hierarchical Multi-scale Knowledge-aware Graph Network (HMKGN) that models multi-scale interactions and spatially hierarchical relationships within whole-slide images (WSIs) for cancer prognostication. Unlike conventional attention-based MIL, which ignores spatial organization, or graph-based MIL, which relies on static handcrafted graphs, HMKGN enforces a hierarchical structure with spatial locality constraints, wherein local cellular-level dynamic graphs aggregate spatially proximate patches within each region of interest (ROI) and a global slide-level dynamic graph integrates ROI-level features into WSI-level representations. Moreover, multi-scale integration at the ROI level combines coarse contextual features from broader views with fine-grained structural representations from local patch-graph aggregation. We evaluate HMKGN on four TCGA cohorts (KIRC, LGG, PAAD, and STAD; N=513, 487, 138, and 370) for survival prediction. It consistently outperforms existing MIL-based models, yielding improved concordance indices (10.85% better) and statistically significant stratification of patient survival risk (log-rank p < 0.05).

zh.title: 分层多尺度图学习与知识引导注意力机制在全切片图像生存分析中的应用
zh.summary: HMKGN提出了一个分层多尺度图网络，用于从全切片图像进行癌症预后分析，同时建模局部细胞相互作用和全局切片级特征。它在生存预测的一致性指数方面比现有的多实例学习方法一贯优越，改进率为10.85%。

fr.title: Apprentissage graphique hiérarchique multi-échelle avec attention guidée par la connaissance pour l'analyse de survie des images entières de lame
fr.summary: HMKGN propose un réseau graphique hiérarchique multi-échelle pour la pronostication du cancer à partir d'images de lames entières, modélisant à la fois les interactions cellulaires locales et les caractéristiques au niveau de la lame mondiale. Il surpasse systématiquement les méthodes existantes d'apprentissage multi-instances avec une amélioration de 10,85% des indices de concordance pour la prédiction de survie.

de.title: Hierarchisches Multi-Scale-Graph-Learning mit wissensgesteuerter Aufmerksamkeit für die Überlebenanalyse von Ganzsichtbildern
de.summary: HMKGN schlägt ein hierarchisches Multi-Scale-Graphen-Netzwerk zur Krebsprognose aus Ganzsichtbildern vor und modelliert sowohl lokale zelluläre Wechselwirkungen als auch globale Merkmale auf Folienebene. Es übertrifft durchgehend bestehende Mehrfach-Instanz-Lernmethoden mit einer Verbesserung von 10,85% bei Konkordanzindizes für die Überlebensprognose.

es.title: Aprendizaje gráfico jerárquico multiescala con atención guiada por el conocimiento para el análisis de supervivencia de imágenes de portaobjetos completos
es.summary: HMKGN propone una red gráfica jerárquica multiescala para la prognosticación del cáncer a partir de imágenes de portaobjetos completos, modelando tanto las interacciones celulares locales como las características a nivel de portaobjetos global. Supera consistentemente los métodos existentes de aprendizaje de múltiples instancias con una mejora del 10,85% en los índices de concordancia para la predicción de supervivencia.

title: Flowette: Flow Matching with Graphette Priors for Graph Generation
summary: Flowette introduces a continuous flow matching framework with graphette structural priors for generative modeling of graphs with recurring motifs. It combines optimal transport coupling with domain-driven constraints to improve generation of complex graph distributions including rings, stars, and trees.
content:
arXiv:2602.23566v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We study generative modeling of graphs with recurring subgraph motifs. We propose Flowette, a continuous flow matching framework, that employs a graph neural network based transformer to learn a velocity field defined over graph representations with node and edge attributes. Our model preserves topology through optimal transport based coupling, and long-range structural dependencies through regularisation. To incorporate domain driven structural priors, we introduce graphettes, a new probabilistic family of graph structure models that generalize graphons via controlled structural edits for motifs like rings, stars and trees. We theoretically analyze the coupling, invariance, and structural properties of the proposed framework, and empirically evaluate it on synthetic and small-molecule graph generation tasks. Flowette demonstrates consistent improvements, highlighting the effectiveness of combining structural priors with flow-based training for modeling complex graph distributions.

zh.title: Flowette: 用于图生成的具有Graphette先验的流匹配
zh.summary: Flowette引入了一个连续流匹配框架，具有graphette结构先验，用于具有重复图案的图的生成建模。它将最优传输耦合与领域驱动的约束相结合，以改进包括环、星形和树形在内的复杂图分布的生成。

fr.title: Flowette : Appariement de flux avec priors Graphette pour la génération de graphes
fr.summary: Flowette introduit un cadre d'appariement de flux continu avec des a priori structurels graphette pour la modélisation générative de graphes avec des motifs récurrents. Il combine l'appariement de transport optimal avec des contraintes pilotées par le domaine pour améliorer la génération de distributions graphiques complexes incluant les anneaux, les étoiles et les arbres.

de.title: Flowette: Flow Matching mit Graphette-Priors für die Graphenerzeugung
de.summary: Flowette führt ein kontinuierliches Flow-Matching-Framework mit Graphette-Strukturpriors für die generative Modellierung von Graphen mit wiederkehrenden Motiven ein. Es kombiniert optimales Transportvorkommen mit domänengesteuerten Einschränkungen, um die Erzeugung komplexer Graphverteilungen, einschließlich Ringe, Sterne und Bäume, zu verbessern.

es.title: Flowette: Coincidencia de flujo con antecedentes de Graphette para la generación de gráficos
es.summary: Flowette introduce un marco de coincidencia de flujo continuo con priors estructurales de graphette para el modelado generativo de gráficos con motivos recurrentes. Combina el acoplamiento de transporte óptimo con restricciones impulsadas por dominio para mejorar la generación de distribuciones gráficas complejas que incluyen anillos, estrellas y árboles.
